frameworks.name: perceptual prediction
frameworks.one_line: ongoing activity is parsed via prediction over learned sequential structure

frameworks.name: self-organizing event segmentation
frameworks.one_line: transient prediction-error spikes drive boundary detection and representation update

frameworks.name: connectionist sequence learning
frameworks.one_line: recurrent statistical learning of within-event regularities

core_question: Can a system that predicts upcoming stimuli use prediction-error spikes to segment continuous activity into discrete events?
key_assumptions: continuous activity contains recurring dynamic patterns
experience yields reliable sequential dependencies within events
stable internal representations support within-event prediction
boundaries can be identified online without supervision
manipulations: timing of representation updates triggered by prediction error
key_findings: learned representations accurately predict upcoming stimuli within an event
transient increases in prediction error mark event boundaries
boundary-triggered updates improve prediction of subsequent activities
interpretations: event segmentation can emerge from perceptual prediction and its failures
self-organized boundary detection refines internal event representations
